reasoning: The informal statement asks to show there is a unique smallest topology on X containing all the topologies T_α. The Lean formalization takes an indexed family `T : I → Set (Set X)` of topologies (via the hypothesis `h : ∀ i, is_topology X (T i)`) and asserts the existence of a unique T' that (a) is a topology, (b) contains every T i, and (c) is contained in every other topology containing all T i. This triple of conditions precisely characterizes "smallest topology containing all T_α": condition (b) says T' contains the family, and condition (c) says T' is minimal among such topologies. The `∃!` quantifier encodes the "unique" requirement, consistent with the informal "unique smallest". Using an indexed family (I → Set (Set X)) instead of an unindexed collection is a standard and logically equivalent encoding of "a family of topologies". No hypotheses are silently added or dropped.
